prompt: Here's my trading journal for the last couple weeks: trade-discipline-suite-workspace/iteration-1/inputs/my_trading_week.csv . Review my week and tell me whether I should size up.

assertions.name: used_expectancy_script
assertions.text: Ran position_sizer.py expectancy --journal on the provided CSV (output shows the JOURNAL EXPECTANCY block or states it executed the script).

assertions.name: expectancy_positive_reported
assertions.text: Reports a positive per-trade expectancy of roughly +0.34R.

assertions.name: concentration_reported
assertions.text: Reports profit concentration (top-3 winners ~84% of gross profit) and flags it as fragile / concentrated.

assertions.name: rule_break_rate_reported
assertions.text: Reports the rule-break rate (~18%, 4 of 22 trades).

assertions.name: verdict_not_scale_up
assertions.text: Verdict is HOLD or do-NOT-scale-up (not an aggressive scale-up), justified by concentration and/or rule-break rate and borderline sample.

assertions.name: caveat_sample_or_costs
assertions.text: Notes the sample-size caveat (~20-30 trades min) and/or that R must be net of costs.
